headline: RM2.27 Billion, 25 Charges, One Verdict, and the Reform Bill Still Unpassed
context: Najib Razak was convicted on all 25 charges over RM2.27 billion from 1MDB on 26 December 2025. The court imposed 15 years jail and an RM11.3879 billion fine. RM24.46 billion in 1MDB-linked debt remains outstanding.

cards.big: Najib was convicted on all 25 charges; the appeal was filed within days. The rally-and-T-shirt response missed the RM24.46 billion 1MDB debt still owed.
cards.sub: The court rejected four Saudi donation letters as forgeries. The conviction did not address the patronage structures that produced the case.

cards.big: The judge declared four Saudi donation letters forgeries. Najib was convicted on all 25 charges.
cards.sub: The fine was RM11.3879 billion for the four abuse-of-power charges (5× gratification). Appeal filed days after the 26 December verdict.

cards.big: Malaysia has paid about RM42 billion servicing 1MDB-linked debt. RM24.46 billion remains outstanding.
cards.sub: RM9.02 billion in sukuk runs to 2039. The remaining debt is larger than a third of the 2026 federal education allocation.

cards.big: Political-financing reform remains unpassed. A draft bill submitted by APPG MPs in July 2022 was never tabled by the government.
cards.sub: Bersih and C4 have repeatedly urged statutory disclosure of donors and spending caps. Anwar's unity government has not tabled the draft.

cards.big: The conviction closes a case. It does not change the financing rules that produced it.
Connected elites across the political spectrum benefit from the absence of donor-disclosure law. Vulnerable households carry the longer-term fiscal cost.
